answer:
The film is modeled as many independent binary asymmetric channels, each with crossover probability \(1 - 100 dA\). The capacity per cell is \(1 - H_b(100 dA)\) bits, and for small \(dA\) this approximates \(100 dA \log_2(1/(100 dA))\). For a film area of 0.1, the number of cells is \(0.1 / dA\), so total capacity tends to \(0.1 \times 100 \log_2(e) \approx 14.4\) bits, but the closest given option is 10.0.

\boxed{J}
